title: Campaign Plan
description: P-B. Ordered milestone backlog for the Campaign loop — a multi-milestone extension of the Delivery Loop (Concept 2), NOT the Auto Loop (Constitution §1.7-E / §3.7). The Campaign runner iterates these milestones in deterministic topological order (depends_on; default linear), driving each through the existing single-sub-sprint Driver, and halts only at human-authority gates. archive/2026-06-20-autonomous-delivery-design.md §5.

campaign_id.description: Path-safe id (no separators, no leading dot) — interpolated into the campaign audit ledger filename; the runner rejects an unsafe value fail-closed.

goal.description: The top-level user-facing goal (以终为始) the campaign decomposes into the milestone backlog.

delivery_mode.description: Explicit topology marker. Campaign plans are executable only when the adopter has opted into delivery_mode=campaign; default human-facing delivery remains single_milestone through the Control Plane + charter.yaml.

signed_by_human.description: P-B campaign-tier gate (campaign_plan_signoff): the Customer signs the milestone backlog before the runner drives it (design §5.1). The runner PAUSES at campaign_plan_signoff until this is true. Enforced by the campaign runner, not the charter validator. LEGACY/pre-F1: a bare top-level signed_by_human:true with NO `signoff` block is treated as pre-F1 (missing snapshot) ⇒ one re-sign required (Δ-19 §3.3.1). When a `signoff` block is present it is authoritative and this flag is ignored.

signoff.description: Δ-19 F1/G1/G4 (design §3.3.1). The signed RESOLVED-scope SNAPSHOT that gives campaign_plan_signoff integrity, so a plan cannot be edited after signoff (remove a milestone, change a future covers_req_ids, or flip an inheriting milestone's resolved acceptance via a charter-default change) while staying 'signed'. OPTIONAL so pre-F1 plans still validate. The runner recomputes the LIVE resolved scope-envelope + hash at load and honors 'signed' ONLY when stored signed_scope_hash == the live hash; a mismatch ⇒ stale ⇒ re-pause at campaign_plan_signoff (re-sign). It adds NO new checkpoint id (B5).

charter_ref.description: Path/ref of the charter resolved against at sign time.

charter_hash.description: Hash of the charter at sign time (G1: a charter-default change that flips an inheriting milestone's resolved acceptance mode changes the envelope ⇒ stale).

scope_envelope.description: The RESOLVED scope at sign time (G4: stored, not just hashed, so prior signed coverage is reconstructable for stale-signoff rendering).

resolved_functional_acceptance.description: The RESOLVED acceptance class {mode, source} (not the literal functional_acceptance which may be absent and inherit the charter default).

isolation_strategy.description: Track-2 T2-B: the per-milestone branch-vs-worktree isolation authority resolved into the envelope ('inherit' when absent), so a post-sign per-milestone strategy edit flips the hash. OPTIONAL so a pre-T2-B snapshot (no isolation_strategy) still validates and is independently flagged stale for the one-time re-sign.

authority.description: Track-2 T2-B: the RESOLVED top-level authority bound into the single signed hash H (budget caps, gap-followup bounds, the merge target trunk_branch, and milestone_isolation), so a post-sign edit that changes the effective authority — auto-remediation extent, the campaign budget, the merge target/gate, or worktree/branch placement/cleanup — flips the hash and the plan goes stale until re-signed. Stored alongside the envelope so signoff_snapshot_authentic reconstructs the SAME H. OPTIONAL so a pre-T2-B snapshot (no authority) still validates and is independently flagged stale for the one-time re-sign.

signed_scope_hash.description: sha256(canonical_json(H)) per the exact hash spec in design §3.3.1 (H = {version:'v1', campaign_id, goal, charter_ref, charter_hash, milestones:[{id,objective,covers_req_ids,subsprint_sequence,depends_on,resolved_functional_acceptance:{mode,source},acceptance_bar},…]}; canonical JSON = UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace, absent arrays normalized to []).

budget.description: Campaign-level COUNTABLE caps (design §5.4a). $ cost is NOT a dimension — no adapter reports per-call cost and subscription harnesses do not expose it (charter budget.max_api_usd caveat). All OPTIONAL; an absent dimension is unbounded.

max_subsprints.description: Max sub-sprints the campaign may dispatch before campaign_budget_exhausted.

max_total_spawns.description: Max cumulative spawns (summed from each run_loop summary's spawn_count).

max_wall_clock_minutes.description: Max cumulative wall-clock minutes (measured via the injected clock).

gap_followup.description: Δ-19 / Constitution §1.7-F clause 2. Per-milestone bounds for the pre-authorized in-envelope completeness-remediation auto-route (the gap-followup dimension, SEPARATE from campaign budget). OPTIONAL; an absent block uses conservative engine defaults (max_subsprints 3, max_no_progress_rounds 1 — a non-shrinking round HALTs on the FIRST occurrence per clause 2/3), and the Phase 2-γ runtime derives an absent-campaign-budget effective-cap from charter.budget.max_fix_rounds_total — the gap-followup dimension is NEVER unbounded.

max_subsprints.description: Max gap-followup remediation sub-sprints the orchestrator may auto-dispatch per milestone before fail-closed escalation to needs_human (§1.7-F clause 2/3).

max_no_progress_rounds.description: Halt-threshold for non-shrinking gap-followup rounds. §1.7-F clause 2/3 requires each round's remaining gap req_id-set to be a strict PROPER SUBSET of the prior round and HALTs on a non-shrinking (non-proper-subset) round — so the default 1 escalates on the FIRST non-shrinking round (no retry). A value > 1 tolerates non-progress and is rejected by charter_validator as a §1.7-D evasion.

trunk_branch.description: The branch milestone work merges INTO at the milestone_merge gate (default main).

milestone_isolation.description: Campaign-tier git isolation at EACH milestone boundary (NOT per sub-sprint). Requires --repo-dir on run_loop. Constitution §1.7-D: strategies here are pre-authorized by the Customer-signed plan; the engine executes them but NEVER auto-merges without a human decision at milestone_merge.

default_strategy.description: Isolation for every milestone unless a milestone overrides isolation_strategy.

branch_name_template.description: Deterministic branch name for new_branch/new_worktree. Tokens: {campaign_id}, {milestone_id}.

worktree_root.description: Parent dir for new worktrees (new_worktree only). Defaults to <repo>-worktrees sibling.

merge_prompt_at_close.description: When true and the milestone used an isolated branch/worktree, PAUSE at milestone_merge before advancing to the next milestone.

cleanup_policy.description: Worktree disposition after a successful merge_now at milestone_merge.

merge_policy.description: Reserved for a future parallel runner; the sequential runner records but does not branch on it.

isolation_strategy.description: DEPRECATED top-level default — prefer milestone_isolation.default_strategy. Values shared/worktree are legacy aliases (shared→current_branch, worktree→new_worktree). Per-milestone override lives on each milestone.

id.description: Path-safe milestone id (it + the sub-sprint id form the per-unit Driver run dir/loop_id).

covers_req_ids.description: Δ-19 (design §3.4). The ONLY writable requirement->milestone coverage map: the Deliver Agent fills it, campaign_plan_signoff signs it. OPTIONAL (every existing plan still validates). Entries are unique + REQ-id-patterned; in Phase 1 a REQ is covered by at-most-one milestone (cross-milestone uniqueness enforced by campaign.py semantic check, not JSON Schema). scope_report derives delivery_status from this signed map joined with the ledger + campaign-state; the ledger never stores coverage.

acceptance_bar.description: One-line statement of what Acceptance must confirm at this milestone's close.

functional_acceptance.description: P-C. Per-milestone acceptance class. NO schema default (absence stays distinguishable from explicit static). Precedence in campaign.derive_milestone_context: an explicit value (incl. 'static') OVERRIDES; absent ⇒ INHERITS charter tooling.acceptance.functional.mode; else static. browser_e2e fires the orchestrator-owned browser evidence gate at this milestone's close. The projected {mode, source} is recorded in derived-context.json. archive/2026-06-20-pc-browser-e2e-design.md §4.2.

subsprint_sequence.description: Ordered (path-safe) sub-sprint ids, UNIQUE within a milestone (the follow-up id-novelty check + per-unit loop_id require it). May be authored up front (Deliver) or filled by the per-milestone full_chain_guided decompose (delivery-loop §4.2.4-G).

depends_on.description: Milestone ids that MUST complete before this one (deterministic topological order). Absent ⇒ linear backlog order. The sequential runner enforces order + lock non-overlap so a future parallel runner is sound.

module_locks.description: Forward-compat: resource paths this milestone owns, for a future parallel runner's isolation. The sequential runner records but does not contend on them.

isolation_strategy.description: Override milestone_isolation.default_strategy for THIS milestone only. inherit = use the plan default.
